posts.text:
Yesterday, Trump said that he’d do whatever is necessary to ease the oil crisis. He also assured America that the crisis “will be over soon.” I'm calling bullshit. Here's why he's trapped in Iran.

robertreich.substack.com/p/p…

The average annual electric bill in the U.S. increased by $110 during Trump’s first year in office.

Remember when he promised to lower energy costs on the campaign trail?

That’s far from the only promise he’s broken.

piped.video/watch?v=AcefFTXU…

Trump repeatedly promised on the campaign trail to cap credit card interest rates at 10 percent.

But since he took office, Americans have paid nearly $240.7B in credit card interest — $134.5B more than what they would have paid under a 10 percent cap.

Like so many of his promises, it was another lie. piped.video/watch?v=vZwV58Dd…

When 3.5% of a population actively participates in sustained, peaceful protest, it can topple authoritarian regimes — like the one currently in the Oval Office.

We need 12 million people to show up on March 28 to get to 3.5%. Will you be one of them?

Find out how at nokings.org

Video

Trump is alone. That’s different from the United States being alone.

May I be so bold as to address myself to America’s allies and friends? I have some thoughts... robertreich.substack.com/p/t…

The AI lobby has spent millions of dollars on today's Democratic primary elections in Illinois.

Why?

To buy itself a Congress that will do its bidding.

Video

Trump is realizing he bit off more than he could chew by attacking Iran and unleashing global economic chaos.

Video

Adam Smith’s masterpiece is one of those rare classics that almost everyone knows about, but a very few have actually read.

It is fundamentally misunderstood — yet it's just as important today as it was in 1776. Let me explain. robertreich.substack.com/p/t…
The war in Iran is an American failure. What do we do now? theguardian.com/commentisfre…

Trump promised a cap on credit card rates.

Instead, he delivered a giveaway to credit card companies at your expense.

Former CFPB Director Rohit Chopra (@ChopraUSA) explains.

Video

Who is Trump’s stupidest cabinet member?

It's a tough call, but there’s a clear winner. robertreich.substack.com/p/t…

The Washington Post is reportedly using readers' personal data to set subscription prices.

It's a tactic known as surveillance pricing. 

Former FTC Chair Lina Khan explains.

Video

The pro-Israel, crypto, and AI lobbies have spent $21M+ to influence tomorrow's Illinois primary via innocuously-named Super PACs like "Elect Chicago Women". 

Not a single one of their ads mentions Israel, crypto, or AI.

Citizens United is corrupting our system every day.

It’s fallen out of the news cycle, but Trump’s FBI and Justice Department are still covering up major details in the Epstein files.

Video
So what does Trump do now that he’s cornered in the Strait of Hormuz? robertreich.substack.com/p/s…

The Pentagon was already one of the biggest culprits of waste, fraud, and abuse.

Now we find out Pete Hegseth has been spending our money on steak and lobster?

Video
Trump Loses His War, Economy, and Mind | The Coffee Klatch with Robert Reich
We Need You on March 28 #nokings
How She Beat Trump (ft. E Jean Carroll)
How Trump's Iran War Ends
Trump Sold You Out to Credit Card Companies (ft. Rohit Chopra)
The Epstein Cover-Up Continues
Hegseth's Lobster Dinner on Your Dime
What Does E. Jean Carroll Think of Trump and Epstein?
World War Trump (Ft. E. Jean Carroll) | The Coffee Klatch with Robert Reich
The World's BEST Sandwich

Stuck in a long security line at the airport? 

Senate Republicans blocked Democrats’ bill to fund DHS agencies that don’t conduct immigration enforcement, like TSA. 

Remember this. https://www.cnn.com/us/live-news/tsa-staff-shortage-delays-shutdown-03-20-26
Cartoon by Mike Luckovich

"Trump has talked to longtime advisers about what it would take to officially make his birthday a national, federal holiday. Similarly, he has already repeatedly inquired about getting a Donald Trump-shaped monument built in our nation's capital…"

zeteo.com/p/donald-tru...

Some House GOP leaders now trying to jump start a second reconciliation bill that would pair spending cuts - like further #Medicaid and ACA Marketplace cuts - with war funding. www.axios.com/2026/03/19/p...

The national debt just hit a record $39 trillion.

Rich Americans used to finance the government by paying taxes.

Now, they finance the government by lending it money and collecting interest payments — profiting as the rest of us pay them back.

It's a big switcheroo you need to understand.

I'm reminded of these wise words from Mr. Fred Rogers on what would have been his 98th birthday. We are all in this together. Let's renew ourselves to the fight for the common good. It's all the more important during these difficult times.
America's media monopoly problem is about to get a whole lot worse.
www.wsj.com/tech/jeff-be...

Jeff Bezos, one of the richest men on earth, is raising $100 billion to replace workers with robots around the world. 

The oligarchs want it all. Not going to happen.

Stand up and FIGHT BACK.

So far the war on Iran has cost $25 billion.

That's $1 billion per day.

$41,666,667 per hour.

$11,574 per second.

Thousands of lives lost.

People don't want this. They want a living wage. They want healthcare. They want to be able to afford a home. They want their basic needs met.

Trump is plotting to shell out nearly $1 billion of taxpayer money to get an energy company to stop building two wind farms.

That will make us more reliant on dirty fossil fuels, whose prices are soaring due to his war on Iran.

Everyone loses — except Trump’s Big Oil donors.
Remember: It’s not about what this country can or can’t afford. It’s about priorities.

Jeff Bezos is worth $220 billion. 

After building that fortune from overworking and exploiting his own workers, Bezos is now developing a fund to acquire manufacturing companies and automate them.

Why do we always hear about immigrants taking jobs when the oligarchs are actually to blame?

The FCC is waiving a rule that bars a single company from owning TV stations reaching more than 39% of U.S. households.

Nexstar and Tegna combined would cover ~60%.

Reminder that Nexstar's CEO has praised Trump and quickly pulled Jimmy Kimmel off its stations last fall.

See how this works?

The national debt just hit a record $39 trillion.

Rich Americans used to finance the government by paying taxes.

Now, they finance the government by lending it money and collecting interest payments — profiting as the rest of us pay them back.

It's a big switcheroo you need to understand.

* U.S. TO DEPLOY THOUSANDS OF ADDITIONAL TROOPS TO THE MIDDLE EAST, THREE U.S. OFFICIALS TELL REUTERS

@reuters.com

Dear allies of America, 

Please don’t confuse our president with us.

Some thoughts...
https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2026/mar/19/donald-trump-american-ally

Donald Trump cannot be trusted to lead America during war. He is unaccountable to the public. He is lawless. He only seeks conflict to cover up for his failures. He cannot offer a coherent justification for what's happening or a plan for what comes next.

When we say "No Kings," this is why.

The FCC is waiving a rule that bars a single company from owning TV stations reaching more than 39% of U.S. households.

Nexstar and Tegna combined would cover ~60%.

Reminder that Nexstar's CEO has praised Trump and quickly pulled Jimmy Kimmel off its stations last fall.

See how this works?

Yesterday, Trump said that he’d do whatever is necessary to ease the oil crisis. He also assured America that the crisis “will be over soon.” I'm calling bullshit. Here's why he's trapped in Iran.

https://robertreich.substack.com/p/playing-it-strait

When 3.5% of a population actively participates in sustained, peaceful protest, it can topple authoritarian regimes — like the one currently in the Oval Office. We need 12 million people to show up on March 28 to get to 3.5%. Will you be one of them? Find out how at nokings.org
Trump is realizing he bit off more than he could chew by attacking Iran and unleashing global economic chaos.
Trump promised a cap on credit card rates. Instead, he delivered a giveaway to credit card companies at your expense. Former CFPB Director Rohit Chopra explains.
It’s fallen out of the news cycle, but Trump’s FBI and Justice Department are still covering up major details in the Epstein files.
The Pentagon was already one of the biggest culprits of waste, fraud, and abuse. Now we find out Pete Hegseth has been spending our money on steak and lobster?
E. Jean Carroll, one of my personal heroes, took on Trump and won. She’s one of the bravest people I’ve met. She shared some thoughts on Trump and Epstein’s awful friendship.
Today I want to tell you about my favorite sandwich, where to get it, and some other very important information you might not have heard before.
We're spending $1 billion per day on war as millions of working people struggle to get by. Meanwhile, America's ultra-rich have never been richer.
It's not just gas prices that are surging thanks to Trump's war. Fertilizer costs could rise too — and send food prices skyrocketing.
Have you seen the commercial that brought down Kristi Noem? It’s even more bizarre than I expected.
